findings.issue: Task 1 description states the implementation in `system_config.py` and `main.py` is already in place and the task only covers writing tests. The tech-spec Task 1 specifies both implementing the delete functions AND writing the tests as a single task. The task files should reflect full implementation scope. If implementation was done outside this task, the tech-spec traceability is broken — the task as written covers only test coverage, not implementation.
findings.fix: Either (a) update Description and What to do to include the implementation steps from tech-spec (add delete functions, add call sites) if the code is not yet written, or (b) if the implementation was done separately, add a note in decisions.md and update the task to reflect the actual remaining scope clearly so reviewers understand the split

findings.issue: Task 3 description states `_monitor_loop()` is already fully implemented and only tests are missing. Like Task 1, the tech-spec specifies implementation + tests together in one task. The task as written covers tests only, which breaks carry-forward traceability from tech-spec.
findings.fix: Same as Task 1 fix (b): if implementation was done outside this task, document it in decisions.md. Update task description to accurately reflect scope (test coverage for existing implementation) and ensure no AC items from tech-spec were dropped

findings.issue: Task 4 description states the implementation already exists and only tests are missing, same pattern as Tasks 1 and 3. The tech-spec specifies adding `_list_firewall_rules_by_prefix()` and replacing `remove_firewall_rules()` body as implementation steps — these are described as pre-done. Carry-forward traceability from tech-spec is incomplete.
findings.fix: Same as Tasks 1 and 3: if implementation was done in a prior session, document that in decisions.md and note it clearly at the top of the task description. Ensure all AC items from tech-spec are present

findings.issue: Required Skills section uses Russian language (`Перед выполнением задачи загрузи`) while all other tasks (1, 3, 4) use English (`Before starting, load`). The template uses Russian but English is expected per project conventions for tech docs.
findings.fix: Change to English: `Before starting, load:` to be consistent with tasks 1, 3, 4

findings.issue: Post-completion checklist uses Russian language (`Записать краткий отчёт в decisions.md по шаблону...`) while tasks 1, 3, 4 use English. Inconsistency within the batch.
findings.fix: Replace Russian bullet text with the English equivalents used in tasks 1, 3, 4: `Write report to decisions.md`, `If deviated from spec — describe deviation and reason`, `Update user-spec/tech-spec if anything changed`

findings.issue: Tech-spec TDD list for Task 1 has 4 entries: `test_delete_ca_key_files_removes_private_key`, `test_delete_ca_key_files_keeps_public_cert`, `test_delete_ca_public_cert_removes_cert`, `test_mitmproxy_works_after_key_deletion`. Task 1's TDD Anchor has 6 entries (adds `test_delete_ca_key_files_idempotent`, `test_delete_ca_public_cert_idempotent`). All 4 tech-spec tests are present — additions are fine. No loss.
findings.fix: No fix needed — this is informational. The extras are appropriate extensions.

findings.issue: What to do step 1 says to write tests in `tests/test_system_config.py` (plural `tests/`), but Task 1's TDD Anchor uses `test/unit/` and `test/` (singular `test/`). The test directory name is inconsistent across tasks within the same batch. This may indicate the wrong path in one of the tasks.
findings.fix: Verify the actual test directory name in the project (`test/` or `tests/`). Align all tasks to use the same path. If `test/` is correct (as used in Tasks 1-3), update Task 4 to use `test/test_system_config.py`

findings.issue: Task 2 declares `depends_on: [4]` and `wave: 2`, which is valid — wave 2 > wave 1 of task 4. However, the dependency rationale in Details mentions task 4 must complete first because `remove_firewall_rules()` is called inside `cleanup()`. This is correct and the wave ordering is consistent. No issue with dependency graph.
findings.fix: No fix needed — dependency and wave are correct.
